questions.question: Explain the difference between class template and function template in C++.
keyPoints: Class templates require explicit type specification during instantiation
Function templates support automatic type deduction
Class templates cannot be overloaded, function templates can
Member functions of class templates are themselves templates

questions.question: What is template specialization? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Template specialization creates specific implementation for particular types
Full specialization applies to one specific type
Partial specialization applies to type patterns like pointers
Example: template<> class Stack<char*> for character pointer stack

questions.question: Write a C++ class template for a Stack with push and pop operations.
keyPoints: Use template<class T> declaration
Include private data member array of type T
Implement push(T item) and pop() functions
Handle stack overflow and underflow conditions

questions.question: What are non-type template parameters? Give an example.
keyPoints: Non-type parameters accept constant values at compile time
Commonly used for specifying array sizes or buffer lengths
Example: template<int size> class Array { T arr[size]; }
Types allowed: integers, pointers, references, enumerations

questions.question: Explain the purpose of the 'typename' keyword in C++ templates.
keyPoints: Used to indicate that a dependent name refers to a type
Required when accessing nested types like iterator or value_type
Distinguishes types from static members or enum values
Preferred over 'class' in modern C++

questions.question: What is explicit template instantiation? When is it useful?
keyPoints: Forces compiler to generate code for specific types at designated points
Syntax: template class Stack<int>;
Useful to reduce compilation time in large projects
Can be used to separate template declaration from definition

questions.question: Differentiate between full specialization and partial specialization with examples.
keyPoints: Full specialization: template<> class Stack<int> - all parameters specified
Partial specialization: template<class T> class Stack<T*> - some parameters specialized
Partial works for type patterns like pointers, arrays, references
Full works only for completely specified types

questions.question: Design and implement a generic class template for a Queue with operations: enqueue, dequeue, isEmpty, isFull. Include template parameters for data type and maximum size.
keyPoints: Declare template<class T, int MAXSIZE> class Queue
Use circular array implementation for efficiency
Implement enqueue(T item) and dequeue() with proper error handling
Include front and rear pointers with circular increment
Show complete C++ code with all member functions
Explain time complexity of operations

questions.question: Explain template template parameters with a suitable example. Show how they differ from regular template parameters.
keyPoints: Template template parameter accepts a template as argument
Example: template<template<typename> class Container, class T>
Demonstrate with STL container like vector
Compare with regular type parameters
Show practical use in generic wrapper classes

questions.question: Write a C++ program to demonstrate member function templates and their specialization. Create a class with both regular and template member functions.
keyPoints: Create class with regular member functions
Add template member function for generic operations
Show how member function templates can be overloaded
Demonstrate with different type instantiations
Explain when explicit specialization of member functions is needed

questions.question: Explain the concept of SFINAE (Substitution Failure Is Not An Error) in C++ templates. Write a program demonstrating its practical use.
keyPoints: SFINAE is a template metaprogramming technique
Explain how invalid substitutions cause overload elimination
Use std::enable_if or type traits to demonstrate
Show practical example: function that works only for integral types
Explain compile-time type checking and overload resolution
